key_insight: Orthonormal low-rank initialization preserves geometry and yields the smallest gap to full fine-tuning in RLVR, making LoRA training more stable than standard or structure-based initializations.
engineering_relevance: For RLVR systems, the initialization of adapter matrices can materially affect both stability and final performance, so geometry-preserving orthonormal init is a practical design choice for parameter-efficient tuning.
method: The authors analyze LoRA under reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards and compare initialization strategies theoretically.
They introduce geometry-preserving orthonormal initialization and instantiate it as two variants: RLPO and RLMO.
They unify the analysis of why PiSSA and MiLoRA may underperform or destabilize training in RLVR.
evidence_signal: Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show orthonormal initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, while PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform.
watch_item: The result depends on RLVR settings and benchmark behavior, so the initialization choice may need revalidation when reward signals, model scale, or task structure change.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
